name: Object CHOP
description: The Object CHOP compares two objects and outputs channels containing their raw or relative positions and orientations. The information that can be output is:
summary: The Object CHOP compares two objects and outputs channels containing their raw or relative positions and orientations. The information that can be output is:

parameters.name: DAT Table
parameters.label: DAT Table
parameters.description: Parameter of this operator. See operator description for details.

parameters.name: Target Object
parameters.label: Target Object
parameters.description: target - The object that is being compared to the position of the reference object. The Target Object can be expressed as a text string. This can be useful when the object name needs to be a variable - it allows you to type in a name which may include expressions or variables.

parameters.name: Reference Object
parameters.label: Reference Object
parameters.description: reference - The object that acts as the origin of the comparison. The  Object can be expressed as a text string.

parameters.name: Swap Target/Reference
parameters.label: Swap Target/Reference
parameters.description: swaptargetreference - Swap the objects defined above in the Target Object and  Object parameters.

parameters.name: Compute
parameters.label: Compute
parameters.description:
compute -  - Specify the information to output from the objects as described in the parameters below. Except for 'measurements', these match the standard transform formats as described by the Transform CHOP.

 transform - A transform using euler (rx ry rz) for the rotation. transformquat - A transform using quaternion (qx qy qz qw) for the rotation. mat - A 4x4 transform matrix. mat3 - A 3x3 transform matrix. This includes scale/rotation but no translation. measure - Enables the toggles below to select what to measure. Measurements give you one object's position relative to another. You get the XYZ of the origin of the Target Object relative to the origin and rotation of the  . That is, you get the XYZ of the target object's origin as if you were at the 0,0,0 location (origin) of the  , looking down the  's Z-axis.

parameters.name: Transform (Euler)
parameters.label: Transform (Euler)
parameters.description: transform - A transform using euler (rx ry rz) for the rotation. transformquat - A transform using quaternion (qx qy qz qw) for the rotation. mat - A 4x4 transform matrix. mat3 - A 3x3 transform matrix. This includes scale/rotation but no translation. measure - Enables the toggles below to select what to measure. Measurements give you one object's position relative to another. You get the XYZ of the origin of the Target Object relative to the origin and rotation of the  . That is, you get the XYZ of the target object's origin as if you were at the 0,0,0 location (origin) of the  , looking down the  's Z-axis.

parameters.name: Position
parameters.label: Position
parameters.description: translate - The displacement from the reference object to the target object.

parameters.name: Rotation
parameters.label: Rotation
parameters.description: rotate - The orientation difference from the reference object to the target object.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description: scale - The scale difference from reference object to the target object.

parameters.name: Quaternion
parameters.label: Quaternion
parameters.description: quat - The quaternion from reference object to the target object.

parameters.name: Bearing
parameters.label: Bearing
parameters.description: bear - The rotation necessary for the reference object to be facing the target object.

parameters.name: Single Bearing Angle
parameters.label: Single Bearing Angle
parameters.description: singlebear - An angle representing where the target object is relative to the reference object. Zero degrees is directly in front, 90 degrees is beside and 180 degrees is behind.

parameters.name: Distance
parameters.label: Distance
parameters.description: distance - The distance between the two objects.

parameters.name: Inverse Square Distance
parameters.label: Inverse Square Distance
parameters.description: invsqr - The inverse squared distance between the two objects, useful for modeling electric forces, audio dropoff and gravity.

parameters.name: Transform Order
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description:
xord -  - The transform order to use for Rotation, Scale, Transform, Bearing, or Single Bearing Angle  modes.

 srt - str - rst - rts - tsr - trs -

parameters.name: Scale Rotate Translate
parameters.label: Scale Rotate Translate
parameters.description: srt - str - rst - rts - tsr - trs -

parameters.name: Rotate Order
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description:
rord -  - The rotation order to use for Rotation, Scale, Transform, Bearing, or Single Bearing Angle  modes.

 xyz - xzy - yxz - yzx - zxy - zyx -

parameters.name: Rx Ry Rz
parameters.label: Rx Ry Rz
parameters.description: xyz - xzy - yxz - yzx - zxy - zyx -

parameters.name: Include Order Channels
parameters.label: Include Order Channels
parameters.description: includeorderchans - Turn on to include channels for Transform Order and Rotate Order.

parameters.name: Bearing Reference
parameters.label: Bearing Reference
parameters.description:
bearingref -  - Bearing requires a direction to use as a reference base.

 x - y - z - vector -

parameters.name: X Axis
parameters.label: X Axis
parameters.description: x - y - z - vector -

parameters.name: Bearing Vector
parameters.label: Bearing Vector
parameters.description:
bearing -  - An arbitrary base direction for the bearing calculation.

 bearingx - bearingy - bearingz -

parameters.name: X
parameters.label: X
parameters.description: bearingx - bearingy - bearingz -

parameters.name: Point Scope X
parameters.label: Point Scope X
parameters.description: tscopex - When one of the optional point inputs is connected, this determines which channels represent X, Y and Z.

parameters.name: Point Scope Y
parameters.label: Point Scope Y
parameters.description: tscopey - When one of the optional point inputs is connected, this determines which channels represent X, Y and Z.

parameters.name: Point Scope Z
parameters.label: Point Scope Z
parameters.description: tscopez - When one of the optional point inputs is connected, this determines which channels represent X, Y and Z.

parameters.name: Append Attributes
parameters.label: Append Attributes
parameters.description: appendattribs - Adds a rotate attribute to any rotation channels the Object  creates.

parameters.name: Smooth Rotation
parameters.label: Smooth Rotation
parameters.description: smoothrotate - When on outputs a smooth rotation curve without graphical jumps at 0, 90, etc.

parameters.name: Channel Names
parameters.label: Channel Names
parameters.description:
nameformat -  - Sets how the created channels are named.

 channel - Automatically names channels. For example: tx, ty, tz. target - Names channels with target prefix. For example: if target = obj1, then obj1:tx, obj1:ty, obj1:tz. reference - Names channels with reference parameter prefix. Behaves like  above but uses the name of the reference object.

parameters.name: Channel Name
parameters.label: Channel Name
parameters.description: channel - Automatically names channels. For example: tx, ty, tz. target - Names channels with target prefix. For example: if target = obj1, then obj1:tx, obj1:ty, obj1:tz. reference - Names channels with reference parameter prefix. Behaves like  above but uses the name of the reference object.

parameters.name: Output Range
parameters.label: Output Range
parameters.description:
outputrange -  - The start and end time of the desired interval of the object path.

 currentframe - Output a single sample at the current frame. timeslice - Span of samples covering the current Time Slice. startend - Uses range defined by the  /  parameters below.

parameters.name: Current Frame
parameters.label: Current Frame
parameters.description: currentframe - Output a single sample at the current frame. timeslice - Span of samples covering the current Time Slice. startend - Uses range defined by the  /  parameters below.

parameters.name: Cook Past Values (slow)
parameters.label: Cook Past Values (slow)
parameters.description: cookpast - If the project has skipped one or more frames, this will attempt to cook it's inputs at multiple previous frames to avoid discontinuities in it's calculations.

parameters.name: Start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: start - The start time of the desired interval of the object path.

parameters.name: Start Unit
parameters.label: Start Unit
parameters.description: startunit - Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: End
parameters.label: End
parameters.description: end - The end time of the desired interval of the object path.

parameters.name: End Unit
parameters.label: End Unit
parameters.description: endunit - Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: Extend Left
parameters.label: Extend Left
parameters.description:
left -  - The extend condition before the  interval. They are:

 hold - Hold the current value of the channel. slope - Continue the slope before the start of the channel. cycle - Cycle the channel repeatedly. mirror - Cycle the channel repeatedly, mirroring every other cycle. default - Use the constant value specified in the Default Value parameter.

parameters.name: Hold
parameters.label: Hold
parameters.description: hold - Hold the current value of the channel. slope - Continue the slope before the start of the channel. cycle - Cycle the channel repeatedly. mirror - Cycle the channel repeatedly, mirroring every other cycle. default - Use the constant value specified in the Default Value parameter.

parameters.name: Extend Right
parameters.label: Extend Right
parameters.description:
right -  - Extend condition after the interval. Same options as Extend Left.

 hold - Hold the current value of the channel. slope - Continue the slope after the end of the channel. cycle - Cycle the channel repeatedly. mirror - Cycle the channel repeatedly, mirroring every other cycle. default - Use the constant value specified in the Default Value parameter.

parameters.name: Default Value
parameters.label: Default Value
parameters.description:
defval - The value used for the Default Value extend condition.	
Note: When creating rotation channels, the Transform CHOP and Object  will select values which minimize frame-to-frame discontinuity. The graphs will appear continuous and free of 180 degree shifts.

parameters.name: Time Slice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: timeslice - Turning this on forces the channels to be "Time Sliced".  A  is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: Scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: scope - To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a  string on the Common page.

parameters.name: Sample Rate Match
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description:
srselect -  - Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Options are not available.

 first - Use rate of first input to resample others. max - Resample to the highest sample rate. min - Resample to the lowest sample rate. err - Doesn't accept conflicting sample rates.

parameters.name: Resample At First Input's Rate
parameters.label: Resample At First Input's Rate
parameters.description: first - Use rate of first input to resample others. max - Resample to the highest sample rate. min - Resample to the lowest sample rate. err - Doesn't accept conflicting sample rates.

parameters.name: Export Method
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description:
exportmethod -  - This will determine how to connect the  channel to the parameter. Refer to the Export article for more information.

 datindex - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the index of the channel in the . datname - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the name of the channel in the . autoname - The channel is the full destination of where to export to, such has geo1/transform1:tx.

parameters.name: DAT Table by Index
parameters.label: DAT Table by Index
parameters.description: datindex - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the index of the channel in the . datname - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the name of the channel in the . autoname - The channel is the full destination of where to export to, such has geo1/transform1:tx.

parameters.name: Export Root
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: autoexportroot - This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by Channel Name is : are relative to.

parameters.name: Export Table
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: exporttable - The  used to hold the export information when using the  Table  Methods (See above).
